feb3a79e02bc930b.body: Small businesses are becoming much easier to check for loans because of new government tracking systems (UDYAM registration and GST data). Micro and small business lending jumped 30.09% this year and now makes up 23.12% of all business loans. But banks struggle to reach these small companies directly because they don't have the right sales networks.
feb3a79e02bc930b.implication: Banks should partner with finance companies that specialize in small business lending through co-lending deals and warehouse financing to tap into this 30% growth market.

4a56445ae52c687a.body: Banks are winning gold loans from finance companies, growing 121% in the last year and staying strong for 8 straight periods. Rising gold prices since 2023 make people want more loans against their gold jewelry. Banks beat finance companies because they have local branches to test gold purity, store it safely, and handle cash payments.
4a56445ae52c687a.implication: Banks with many branches should start or expand gold loan services now while gold prices stay high.

010ca380b2d9a684.body: Small business lending is growing 30.09% per year because government registration systems (UDYAM and GST) now provide the data banks need to evaluate tiny borrowers. Micro and small businesses now make up 23.12% of all business lending, up from much lower levels. Banks that built systems to use this government data over the past 2-3 years are winning big, but the window to catch up is closing.
010ca380b2d9a684.implication: Banks should immediately invest in technology that can automatically analyze GST cash flows and UDYAM business data to approve small business loans quickly.

666b500886d66da0.body: Big companies getting PLI scheme money are spending more on their suppliers, with credit growing 17-35% in key manufacturing sectors like engineering, metals, and chemicals. Banks can lend to these suppliers more safely than normal small business loans because the big PLI company will pay them back. This turns risky small business lending into safer corporate-backed lending.
666b500886d66da0.implication: Banks should offer supply chain finance products to suppliers of PLI scheme companies instead of standalone small business loans.

111782e8659e3a41.body: In October 2024, RBI changed the rules about which housing loans count toward banks' required social lending quotas. Some banks suddenly had way more loans that qualified, with housing social lending growing 39.94% year-over-year. Banks that got this unexpected surplus can now sell certificates to other banks that are behind on their social lending targets.
111782e8659e3a41.implication: Banks with extra social lending credits should sell PSL Certificates to banks that need them - it's free money from a rule change.

93ce5ef0317a1dc1.body: Big infrastructure projects that banks funded from 2022-2024 are finishing up, with some areas down 26% and 44% while others still grow at 21% and 74%. Banks have teams that know how to finance large projects like roads and bridges. The government wants more solar, wind, and green hydrogen projects built, creating a clear pipeline of deals to replace the old infrastructure loans.
93ce5ef0317a1dc1.implication: Banks should point their existing project finance teams toward renewable energy deals instead of traditional infrastructure.

9bef6e4e00965a35.body: Services companies are borrowing money 18.6% faster than last year and now make up 28.2% of all business credit in India. NBFCs (non-bank lenders) are also growing again at 16.7% after regulatory troubles, and they're the main way banks reach services companies. Services credit growth jumped by 7 percentage points this year, making it the fastest-growing major sector.
9bef6e4e00965a35.implication: Banks should either build direct lending teams for services companies or lend more money to NBFCs who already know how to reach this fast-growing sector.

84272ac3d4be9404.body: Credit cards are growing fast at 8.2% per year and reached 119.4 million cards in April 2026. Government banks only have 24% of all credit cards, but they control 64% of debit cards — that's a huge gap. Government banks have millions of people who already use their debit cards but don't have credit cards yet.
84272ac3d4be9404.implication: Government banks should offer credit cards to their existing debit card customers since they already have the banking relationship and know these customers.

395b93576adcd5b5.body: Micro ATMs dropped 7.17% this year but there are still 1.37 million of them running. Rural areas need cash access but full ATMs cost too much to set up there. Micro ATMs let banks provide cash services at much lower cost than building branches or full ATMs.
395b93576adcd5b5.implication: Banks should expand business correspondent networks with micro ATM placement in rural areas instead of expensive full ATM rollouts.

a89fc5166d8d7d11.body: Credit card spending in the 'other' category exploded 493% in value and 300% in volume this year. This massive growth comes from new payment systems being built into public transport (tap-to-pay on buses and trains) and apps that now let people use credit cards through UPI. People are using credit cards in completely new places where they never could before.
a89fc5166d8d7d11.implication: Banks should quickly connect their credit cards to these new payment systems to capture their share of this fast-growing market.

89c7529fe2a1efe4.body: Credit card spending is slowing down now, which means credit card balances will grow more slowly in the coming months. When people spend less on their cards today, banks see smaller balance increases later.
89c7529fe2a1efe4.implication: Banks should expect slower credit card portfolio growth and adjust their lending targets and marketing spend accordingly.
